report.title: `AccessPointTable` used `std::sync::RwLock` with `.unwrap()` everywhere, poisoning the access-point lookup on any in-guard panic
report.summary: Every read/write on the per-`ProtocolEntry` `AccessPointTable` went through `self.inner.read().unwrap()` / `self.inner.write().unwrap()` against a `std::sync::RwLock`. `std::sync::RwLock` poisons when a thread panics while holding the guard; once poisoned, every subsequent `read()` / `write()` returns `Err(PoisonError)` and the call site's `unwrap()` panics — turning a single in-guard panic into a permanent process-wide failure of every `Client::request_fn`, `register`, `insert`, `remove`, `len`, `names` call against that table.

impact: A single panic that happens while any thread is holding the `AccessPointTable` guard (most plausibly during the `Drop` of a stored `AccessPoint<C, TS>` triggered by `refresh_path`'s `*slot = node.clone()` reassignment, or from an unwinding in a downstream type's `Drop`) poisons the lock for the lifetime of the `Server` / `Client`. From that moment on, every named-endpoint lookup (`Client::request_fn(name, ...)`, `serve` paths reaching the table, registration calls, introspection helpers) panics on the `.unwrap()`. Because each lookup runs inside a Tokio task, the panic aborts the request-handling task — the connection drops without a response, and the server keeps accepting new connections that will panic on the same unwrap, indefinitely. Crate-wide convention in `src/alias.rs` already mandates `PRwLock` (parking_lot) for exactly this reason; `AccessPointTable` was the lone std-RwLock holdout.

behavior_diff.expected: An in-guard panic on one request should not affect any other request. Lookups against `AccessPointTable` should never panic on lock-state alone; the only allowable panic surface is the user's own code path running outside the lock.
behavior_diff.actual: After any in-guard panic, every subsequent `self.inner.read().unwrap()` / `.write().unwrap()` returns `Err(PoisonError)` from std's `RwLock` and `.unwrap()` panics. The 8 call sites in `src/executable/access/table.rs` (lines 66, 78, 95, 99, 103, 107, 111, 117 in 0.8.1) are all reachable from the public API surface (`insert`, `refresh_path`, `get`, `contains`, `remove`, `len`, `is_empty`, `names`) and every named-endpoint dispatch panics permanently.

status.fix: Replaced `std::sync::RwLock` with `crate::alias::PRwLock` (re-export of `parking_lot::RwLock`) in `src/executable/access/table.rs`. parking_lot's `RwLock` does not poison — guards are returned directly, not wrapped in `Result`. All 8 `.read().unwrap()` / `.write().unwrap()` call sites were simplified to `.read()` / `.write()`. The crate's own `src/alias.rs` already documents `PRwLock` as the mandatory choice for all internal code (`All internal code MUST import from this module`), so this change aligns `AccessPointTable` with the rest of the crate. No public API surface changed.
